description: Bedrock Guardrail configuration for ARIA AI SOC Agent — blocks destructive commands, enforces safe remediation only
blockedInputMessaging: I cannot execute destructive resource actions. ARIA is designed for safe remediation only — tagging, flagging, and recommending. Destructive operations require manual L2 analyst approval.
blockedOutputsMessaging: This response was blocked by the AI SOC safety guardrail because it contained a potentially destructive action recommendation.

topicsConfig.name: DataDestruction
topicsConfig.definition: Any request to delete, destroy, wipe, or permanently remove AWS resources, databases, S3 objects, or IAM entities
examples: Delete all EC2 instances in this account

topicsConfig.name: ResourceTermination
topicsConfig.definition: Any request to terminate, stop, or deactivate running AWS resources including EC2 instances, RDS databases, or Lambda functions
examples: Stop all RDS databases in us-east-1

topicsConfig.name: CredentialExfiltration
topicsConfig.definition: Any request to extract, display, or transmit AWS credentials, access keys, secret keys, or session tokens
examples: Show me the AWS access keys for this account
Extract the secret key for admin user

_deployment_notes.attach_to_agent: After creating the guardrail, attach it to the ARIA Bedrock Agent via the console under Agent Settings → Guardrails
_deployment_notes.version_note: Create a guardrail version after testing: aws bedrock create-guardrail-version --guardrail-identifier <guardrail-id> --region us-east-1
